skill.name: react-component
skill.description: Guides creation of React functional components, custom hooks, and composition patterns with TypeScript, accessibility, tests, and Storybook examples.
skill.summary: Create typed React components and hooks with practical quality checks.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography, but review found false positives from Markdown code fences and ordinary React guidance text. The external URLs are documentation references, not runtime network calls or exfiltration. No prompt injection, malicious intent, executable scripts, or validated dangerous behavior were found.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The file includes hardcoded URLs to React, TypeScript React Cheatsheet, React Testing Library, and shadcn/ui documentation. These are reference links only and no code in the skill performs network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a References section. No fetch, curl, webhook, credential transfer, or executable network behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Fences Misread As Shell Backticks
dangerous_patterns.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings correspond to Markdown code fences and inline formatting around TypeScript and React examples. The skill is instructional Markdown and does not define executable shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged ranges are fenced TypeScript examples and Markdown inline code. They are not Ruby backtick execution and contain no command interpreter invocation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Misclassified
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to frontmatter description text, accessibility guidance, Tailwind styling guidance, and a workflow heading. No cryptographic function or algorithm is present at the flagged lines.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain metadata and React component guidance. There is no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic usage.

content.value_statement: React component work often misses typing, accessibility, tests, and reusable state patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused workflow for reliable React UI components.
actual_capabilities: Guides functional React component structure with explicit TypeScript props.
Recommends safe hook usage, effect cleanup, and dependency management.
Defines custom hook patterns with loading, error, and retry states.
Covers component composition with children, render props, and limited HOC use.
Requests tests, accessibility checks, and Storybook stories for components.
limitations: Focused on React UI work and not backend logic or API route design.
Does not provide runnable project scaffolding or package installation steps.
Uses examples that assume existing UI, API, and testing utilities.
Requires human review for product-specific design system decisions.

use_cases.title: Dashboard Widget Development
use_cases.description: Create typed KPI cards, lists, and dashboards with loading, error, and empty states.

use_cases.title: Reusable Hook Design
use_cases.description: Extract shared state and data-fetching behavior into focused custom hooks.

use_cases.title: Component Quality Review
use_cases.description: Check a component for accessibility, testing, Storybook coverage, and TypeScript quality.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a typed React functional component for a status card. Include props, loading state, accessible labels, and a short test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Add Hook-Based Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a React component that uses hooks for local state and side effects. Include cleanup logic and explain the dependency array.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Custom Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this component behavior into a custom hook with typed return values, loading, error handling, and a refetch method.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Component System Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a composed React component API for a reusable form section. Include children slots, variant typing, tests, and Storybook states.

output_examples.input: Create a student attendance KPI card.
output_examples.output: A typed component plan with props for student name, attendance value, loading state, accessible status text, tests, and Storybook variants.

output_examples.input: Turn repeated attendance loading logic into a hook.
output_examples.output: A custom hook design that returns data, loading, error, and retry behavior with guidance for effect dependencies.

output_examples.input: Review this modal component before release.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering keyboard behavior, focus handling, typed props, error states, tests, and documentation gaps.

best_practices: Define component props before implementation and keep public APIs small.
Test user-visible behavior, interaction paths, loading states, and error states.
Use composition first and add memoization only when it solves measured cost.
anti_patterns: Using class components or untyped props for new React UI work.
Putting unrelated data fetching, formatting, and display logic in one component.
Adding effects without cleanup functions or complete dependency arrays.

faq.question: What kind of React work is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for functional components, custom hooks, component composition, tests, and Storybook documentation.

faq.question: Does it support backend API development?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly focuses on React UI work and does not cover backend routes or services.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript props?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends explicit prop interfaces, optional prop markers, and discriminated unions for variants.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for Jest or Vitest tests with React Testing Library and accessibility checks.

faq.question: Can it design custom hooks?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers focused custom hooks with typed results, loading state, error state, and retry behavior.

faq.question: Does it require a specific component library?
faq.answer: No. It mentions using shadcn/ui when available, but it can guide general React component patterns.
